programs.name: CHOICES
programs.what_it_does: Connecticut's State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP) that provides free information, counseling, and assistance to seniors about Medicare, Medicaid, Medicare Supplement Insurance (Medigap), and Long Term Care Insurance. Volunteers are available to meet with seniors throughout Connecticut.
programs.who_its_for: Medicare-eligible individuals, their families, and caregivers
programs.official_url: Contact through Area Agencies on Aging or call 1-800-994-9422

programs.name: Connecticut Home Care Program for Elders (CHCPE)
programs.what_it_does: A cornerstone program offering a wide array of non-medical support services to help seniors 65 and older remain in their own homes. The program extends beyond medical care to include social support and community-based services.
programs.official_url: Information available through Area Agencies on Aging

programs.name: Alzheimer's Respite Care Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers relief to stressed caregivers by providing information, support, care plan development, and respite services for individuals with Alzheimer's disease or related dementia. Maximum of $3,500 available per year and up to 30 days of out-of-home respite care services annually.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of individuals with Alzheimer's disease or related dementia
programs.official_url: Administered by the Department on Aging
programs.benefit_value: Up to $3,500 per year; maximum 30 days respite care annually

programs.name: Connecticut Supplemental Income Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides additional cash assistance beyond the Federal SSI amount to eligible seniors and disabled individuals. The program does not require actual receipt of SSI.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Individuals 65 years or older, blind, or disabled who meet income and asset guidelines
programs.official_url: Contact local Department of Social Services office
programs.benefit_value: State cash supplement to federal SSI

programs.name: CT Energy Assistance Program (CEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides utility and housing assistance to eligible low-income households. The program helps with energy costs and related housing needs.[5]
programs.official_url: Information available through Area Agencies on Aging

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Assists eligible homeowners with weatherization improvements to reduce energy costs and improve home efficiency.[5]
programs.official_url: Information available through Area Agencies on Aging
